concepts.title: Behaviour of Grignard Reagent as Base
concepts.conceptBody:
Chemistry Of Grignard Reagent

Victor Grignard discovered that a dry alkyl halide will react with dry magnesium metal in a dry ether solvent to produce an organometallic compound which behaves as if it has the structure R $\text{Mg}-\text{X}$ It is now called an alkylmagnesium halide or Grignard reagent:

$\text{R}-\text{X}+\text{Mg}\xrightarrow{dry\,\,ether}\text{R}-\text{Mg}-\text{X}$

R = alkyl (all types) or vinyl or alkynyl or aryl X = I, Br, Cl

Grignard Reagent can act both as a base and as a nucleophile. It happens due to the polar nature of grignard reagent

${{\text{R}}^{8-}}-\text{M}{{\text{g}}^{8+}}\text{X}$

Also in the preparation of grignard reagent, the reactivity of halides is as follows

$\text{I}>\text{Br}>\text{Cl}>\text{F (though F is very less reactive) }$

Grignard Reagent as Base

As a base it will try to abstract hydrogen from other compounds like ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$, alcohols, amines terminal alkynes etc. to form alkanes

concepts.title: Behaviour of Grignard Reagent as Nucleophile
concepts.conceptBody:
Gridnard Reagent as Nucleophile

As a nucleophile it will attack electrophiles. Let us take its reaction with compounds like aldehydes & ketones, esters, acid halides etc to understand it. Also we take into consideration that which reaction is step up (i.e increase in no. of carbon) or step down (i.e. decrease in no. of carbon)

concepts.title: With Aldehydes
concepts.conceptBody:
Reaction With aldehydes:

With aldehydes. $\text{RMgX}$ gives alcohols and its a Step up reaction.

mechanism is simple

1- alcohol are only given by formaldehyde

concepts.title: With Ketones
concepts.conceptBody:
Reaction With Ketones

We have $1\,\,\And \,\,2$ alcohol as product from aldehydes, so for 3 alcohols Ketones are used. Ketones on reaction with grignard reagent gives 3 alcohol.

This is also a STEP UP reaction

concepts.title: With Carbon Dioxide
concepts.conceptBody:
Reactions With $\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

This is also a STEP UP reaction & used in formation of acids.

So far

concepts.title: With Esters
concepts.conceptBody:
Reactions With esters

It leads to formation of ketones & further to 3 alcohol

You have to be careful of ${{R}^{\prime }}\,\,\And \,\,{{R}^{\prime \prime }}$ as ${{R}^{\prime \prime }}$ is replaced by $R$. So step up & step down will depend upon $R\,\,\And \,\,{{R}^{\prime \prime }}$

For aldehydes $\text{HCOO{R}'}$ is used

Note: \[OR''\] of ester does not appear in aldehyde or ketone.
